steps.title: Track the location effect
steps.content: The passage gives two opposite effects of crab burrowing. In interior salt pannes, burrowing loosens soil, improves drainage, and helps plants move back in as the pannes shrink. At marsh edges, the same kind of soil loosening can accelerate erosion and promote marsh loss. The key variable is location.

steps.title: Reject wrong variables
steps.content: Choice A makes panne size the issue, but the passage contrasts interior pannes with marsh edges. Choice C discusses where burrowing is concentrated, not what burrowing does. Choice D invents a link between burrowing intensity and relative panne size. Choice B captures the location-based contrast between plant increases and plant decreases. The answer is B.
